hypothesis: If fragment-based and combined diversity constraints, including adaptive penalization of overrepresented structures, are applied during REINVENT4 reinforcement learning for singlet-fission molecular design, then the generated molecules will exhibit greater chemical diversity—lower mean pairwise similarity, more clusters, higher unique-fragment percentages, and fewer overrepresented patterns—while retaining comparable predicted singlet-fission scores compared to an unconstrained baseline, because the constraints penalize mode collapse and promote exploration.
